districts.name: Barrowcleft
districts.summary: Residences and markets for the farmers who work the radiant-energy fields and eeleries.
districts.description: Barrowcleft is the home to the laborers and overseers of the Ministry of Preservation who attend the radiant energy farms of Doskvol. It is a dusty, rural district, with simple wooden buildings of only one or two stories and wide dirt roads to accommodate large cargo wagons. The farmers of Barrowcleft are organized into tight-knit family-based clans that are proud of their vital role in the city's welfare and hold themselves apart from the "city folk" across the river. Outsiders are welcome here for honest trade, but are met with a cold suspicion otherwise.

landmarks.name: Barrow Bridge
landmarks.description: One of several residential bridges in the city. The bridge is lined with homes, shops, and merchant stalls. Families of river-fishers work from ramshackle wooden huts along the banks on either side. They hunt the large and dangerous wild river eels that gather to feed on refuse near the channel to the sea.

landmarks.name: Lightning Tower
landmarks.description: The lightning towers of Doskvol are marvels of electroplasmic engineering, requiring constant attention from the powerful Sparkwrights guild. The largest towers are over 200 feet tall and include their own internal generators to provide power to the lightning barrier that keeps the ravenous spirits of the deathlands out of the city.

landmarks.name: Barrowcleft Market
landmarks.description: This open-air marketplace provides a place for the radiant energy farms to sell fresh produce and goods made from their crops. Other related vendors have also sprung up here, including brewers and distillers, weavers, dyers, and goat breeders. The tough and close-knit people of Barrowcleft have managed to keep criminal influence out of their market and it's famous as a rare place for fair trade in the city.

landmarks.name: Radiant Energy Farm
landmarks.description: The wondrous power of radiant energy allows crops to grow in the darkness of Duskwall. Life in the city depends upon these farms, so their delicate radiant plants and irrigation systems are watched constantly by specially appointed deputies of the Watch.

districts.scene: Farmers trudging to and from work. Tradespeople crafting simple goods. Merchants selling their wares. Heavy-laden cargo wagons transporting food into the city. Vigilant deputies surveying the fields from their watchtowers.
districts.streets: Smooth dirt roads, drainage ditches. Black Hill Road, Mill Street, Porter Street.
districts.buildings: Low, wide wooden structures. Barns. Animal pens. Stone mills. Hilltop manors for the Overseers. Crowded apartments, towers, and market stalls along Barrow Bridge.

npcs.name: Chief Prichard
npcs.role: The Head Overseer of Labor for the Ministry of Preservation in Duskwall. Manages the workers and food allotments for the city districts.

npcs.name: Hester Vale
npcs.role: Matriarch of the oldest farm family. The living embodiment of "tough but fair."

npcs.name: Mara Keel
npcs.role: A former smuggler who's gone into hiding among the farm laborers of Barrowcleft.

districts.special_rule: Barrowcleft market is one of the best marketplaces in the city, but criminal types draw lots of unwanted attention. You can take +1d to acquire an asset here, but also accrue +2 heat.

districts.name: Brightstone
districts.summary: The grand mansions and luxury shops of the wealthy elite.
districts.description: Brightstone is home to many of the wealthiest and most influential citizens of Doskvol. Its streets are broad and paved, under bright electric lights; its canals are sparkling and clean, with perfumed water; its houses are all of fine, pale marble blocks, rich timbers, and intricate ironworks. There are cultivated parks fed by radiant energy; lavish restaurants and cafes; jewelers, tailors, and other luxury shops. Street-side vendors are forbidden here, resulting in a serene, spacious atmosphere, punctuated by the occasional carriage or marching Bluecoat patrol.

landmarks.name: Unity Park
landmarks.description: A grand park, fountain, and roundabout featuring an enormous monument commemorating Imperial victory in the Unity War (or the War for Skovlan Independence, depending on who you talk to).

landmarks.name: Silver Market
landmarks.description: A curated open-air emporium overlooking the North Hook channel. Named for its original use as the primary marketplace for silver traders, it's now host to luxury goods vendors of all sorts, including rare Iruvian silks, spices from the Dagger Isles, horse-traders, carriage upholsterers, and rare alchemical distillations (including some illegal spirit essences--to which the City Watch turns a blind eye).

landmarks.name: The Sanctorium
landmarks.description: The chief cathedral dedicated to the Church of the Ecstasy of the Flesh. It's a towering edifice of buttresses and spires, originally commissioned by the Emperor during his last visit to Doskvol nearly 500 years ago. Devotees gather weekly to purify themselves in baptismal rites and through the ritual destruction of rogue spirits in electroplasm. The catacombs beneath contain the cremated ash of many famous and affluent citizens.

landmarks.name: Bowmore Bridge
landmarks.description: This massive structure of bright white stone and shimmering metal-work is said to be the largest bridge in the Imperium. Luxury apartments and shops perch all along its length from Brightstone to Whitecrown.

districts.scene: Elite Bluecoat patrols, arrayed in fine armor and gleaming gun-pikes. Wealthy citizens strolling through manicured parks, attended by servants. Horse-drawn coaches and the occasional electroplasmic carriage rumbling along the avenues.
districts.streets: Broad, clean, well-lit. Silvermark Avenue, Goldcrest Avenue, Ruby Street, Sapphire Street.
districts.buildings: Pale stone mansions with lightning barriers, grand manor houses, lavish townhouses, opulent theaters and restaurants, luxury shops.

npcs.name: Lord Strangford
npcs.role: Operates one of the largest leviathan hunter fleets, serves on the City Council and is a high-ranking member of the secret order within the Church of Ecstasy.

npcs.name: Commander Bowmore
npcs.role: Chief Officer of the Watch in Brightstone. Bowmore's family financed Bowmore Bridge centuries ago and now holds many positions of power.

npcs.name: Rolan Wott
npcs.role: An influential magistrate who handles property, endowments, and financial cases. Famous for his extravagant parties.

districts.special_rule: Most engagement rolls suffer -1d due to heavy Bluecoat patrols. Operations against the nobility in Brightstone are considered on "hostile turf " for the purpose of generating heat.

districts.name: Charhollow
districts.summary: A crowded district of tenements and stacked houses, home to the city's working class.
districts.description: This crowded district is home to the bulk of the workforce of the city--servants, dockers, sailors, stockyard and eelery workers, cabbies, and so on. It's cheap, noisy, cramped, and sweltering from cookfires and hissing steam-pipes, but there's a familial camaraderie among its residents that you won't find anywhere else. The people of Charhollow are a true community, brought together by circumstance, but bound by ties of mutual support and care in stark contrast to the cutthroat ruthlessness that constitutes business as usual in the rest of the city.

landmarks.name: The Sheets
landmarks.description: Washers, tailors, and seamstresses congregate in this neighborhood, filling the alleyways between the buildings with the billowing fabrics of their trade. A secret association of anarchists among the working class meets here to plot their schemes for revolution.

landmarks.name: Strangford House
landmarks.description: The grand, fenced estate of the powerful Strangford family perches on the hill of a private island overlooking Charhollow. Many who live in the district toil in Strangford's factories and workhouses, and few miss the chance to throw the evil eye in their direction when they catch glimpse of their house on the hill.

landmarks.name: Charhollow Market
landmarks.description: A public market fills the open square here, offering fair prices and decent goods to the local community.

landmarks.name: Kellen's
landmarks.description: One of the oldest pubs in the city, with a dizzying selection of Skovlander ales and whiskeys. Rich and poor alike rub elbows here to enjoy the traditional food and music with their drinks, though recently, the pub has become the target of masked anti-Skovlander bigots, who've vandalized the property and assaulted some patrons, shouting "No Skovs!" and "Skovs go home!"

districts.scene: Laborers returning from work shout greetings to friends and families. Groups of people cook and eat together at communal cook-fires. Children run wild, playing at hunt-and-peek and catch-the-ghost.
districts.streets: Steep inclines cut with crude stone stairs, twisting alleyways, dirt and cobblestones. Bridge Road, Canal Street, Hill Street.
districts.buildings: Stacked one- or two-room homes, cheap tenements, ramshackle apartments, well-worn taverns and public houses.

npcs.name: Hutton
npcs.role: A Skovlander refugee and former soldier, now the leader of an anarchist revolutionary movement, bent on forcing the government to acknowledge Skovlander rights in the Empire.

npcs.name: Briggs
npcs.role: The owner of a merchant stall at Charhollow market, cover for a network of gossips, spies, and code-smiths among the working class people of the district, selling their services to those who need them.

npcs.name: Corben
npcs.role: An ex-military Skovlander on the lam for crimes against the empire.

districts.special_rule: Operations against the citizenry in Charhollow are considered on "hostile turf " for the purpose of generating heat.

districts.name: Charterhall
districts.summary: The city's civic offices and the hub for shops, artisans, and commerce.
districts.description: Charterhall is the site of the first major construction in the city, in the days before the cataclysm. The old wall upon which was built the first lightning barrier in the Empire still stands in partial ruin around the district. The area is now home to the civic offices of the government including the courts, licensing and taxation offices, banks, and records archives. City officials and students at Charterhall University live here, along with the captains of Imperial industry who prefer to reside within sight of their fortunes.

landmarks.name: Charter Wall
landmarks.description: Along the ruins of the old walls are a sprawl of artist colonies. Bohemian lovers of music and sculpture, these students are typically patronized by a single individual or family who expect their charges to master their craft and make art for their edification.

landmarks.name: Bellweather Crematorium
landmarks.description: The site of the spirit bells and the rookery for the deathseeker crows. Bodies recovered by the Spirit Wardens are incinerated in electroplasm here to destroy their ghosts.

landmarks.name: Clerk Street
landmarks.description: The main avenue of the district is lined with imposing governmental structures of all sorts, tucked behind iron fences, patrolled by a mix of Bluecoats on the sidewalks and mounted Imperial cavalry on the grounds.

landmarks.name: Jayan Park
landmarks.description: The great alchemist for whom this park is named contrived to formulate soil and seeds that could produce real, growing trees, without sunlight or radiant energy. They are horrifically toxic to all living things and must not be touched, but they still grow beautifully here, over 100 years later.

landmarks.name: Charterhall University
landmarks.description: A dozen buildings have been converted into classrooms and dormitories for the students of this modest-seeming but nevertheless prestigious institution. The school's massive Sparkwright Tower, where experts of spark-craft are trained, looms huge over the district, often belching fire and smoke from the more vigorous lessons.

districts.scene: Clerks and government workers rush to and fro, official papers bulging from their valises. Wealthy bankers trundle past in heavy carriages with private bodyguards arrayed in clanking armor. Students gather at street-corner cafes to discuss Iruvian politics, the tribal lineages of the Dagger Isles, and other esoteric matters.
districts.streets: Broad, clean, well-lit. Clerk Street, Jayan Way, Dalmore Avenue, Imperial Avenue.
districts.buildings: Imposing stone buildings with officious columns and classical sculptural motifs.

npcs.name: Lady Drake
npcs.role: A magistrate who is "reasonable" when it comes to street crime, so long as the offender's purse is sufficient.

npcs.name: Lord Penderyn
npcs.role: Chief Scholar of the Archive of Echoes, authorized by the Emperor to keep a collection of ancient ghosts trapped in spirit bottles, to be consulted in cases where knowledge from the distant past would benefit the operation of the Imperial government. Lord Penderyn also consults the spirits on his own volition, forming the rebellious Path of Echoes society for other elites and nobles who seek communion with the spectral realm.

districts.special_rule: The records in Charterhall can be of particular interest to criminal sorts. Take a Devil's Bargain for +1d to gather info here in exchange for 1 heat (the Bluecoats are always watching for scoundrels like you).

districts.name: Coalridge
districts.summary: Industrial laborers and factories, cramped and soot-choked.
districts.description: Coalridge is home to most of the machinists, industrial laborers, and factories of the city. It's cramped, soot-choked, and loud--spewing dense clouds of black smoke, showers of sparks, and burning cinders. The old elevated train lines that once hauled coal now carry heavy equipment and raw materials to and from Gaddoc Station, though many of the ancient tracks and cars have been abandoned to squatters who've converted them into makeshift homes.

landmarks.name: Coalridge Mine
landmarks.description: The site of the first permanent settlement at the river delta, the mine was originally built by the ancient Skov kingdom, who called it Doskovol-- literally, "The Skov's Coal." The mine still operates over 1000 years later, though demand for coal has dropped sharply as the Imperium adopts electroplasmic power more and more widely.

landmarks.name: The Old Rail Yard
landmarks.description: Before Gaddoc Station was built, this industrial rail yard was a center for commerce in the city. The Old Yard now serves only a couple heavy cargo trains daily, with many of its old rail cars rusted in place where they were abandoned.

landmarks.name: The Ironworks
landmarks.description: The Ironworks is a sprawling collection of massive industrial workhouses. Cruel foremen drive indentured laborers around the clock to keep up with the massive production demands to replace and refit leviathan hunter ships as well as the need for goods transported out to the Imperium at large.

landmarks.name: Brickston
landmarks.description: The most densely packed residential area in Duskwall. Brickston is a cramped jumble of multi-story brick row houses, stacked one atop the other. Many of the toughest scoundrels of the underworld hail from here, learning the harsh lessons of survival and gang life within its dark maze.

districts.scene: Soot-covered workers hacking up black bile as they trudge home from the factories. Heavy rail cargo being unloaded by crane. Streettough waifs running wild. A factory boss lashing a worker for an infraction. Squatters cooking a meal in the coal engine of an abandoned train car.
districts.streets: Multi-level, crowded with crates and discarded junk. Elevated rail lines. Dunvil Way, Black Rock Road, Wright Street, Crane Street, Forge Street.
districts.buildings: Tall and narrow brick row houses with belching chimneys, metal-clad factories and warehouses, train cars converted into dwellings.

npcs.name: Master Slane
npcs.role: A notorious factory foreman known for excessive and cruel punishment. Many attempts have been made on his life, but all have failed. Some say he's a devil.

npcs.name: Belle Brogan
npcs.role: A Skovlander factory worker gaining popularity as a union organizer. It's only a matter of time before a factory boss tries to make an example of her.

npcs.name: Hopper
npcs.role: A drug addict, Whisper, and all-around weirdo who perches on rooftops in the district. Hopper claims to see "spirit train tracks" stretching beyond the horizon.

districts.special_rule: Because the factories of Coalridge operate around the clock, there's no ideal time for clandestine crime here, but foremen are happy to be bribed to "take a break" or look the other way.

districts.name: Crow's Foot
districts.summary: A cramped neighborhood of multi-level streets, ruled by gangs.
districts.description: Crow's Foot is a crossroads, merging many qualities of its neighboring districts: the illict vices of Silkshore, the labor and trade of the Docks, the poverty of Charhollow, and the classic architecture of Charterhall. The district is a patchwork, both held together and threatened to be torn apart by the menagerie of competing street gangs and Bluecoat squads that claim every avenue and corner as territory in an endless turf war.

landmarks.name: Crow's Nest
landmarks.description: An ancient tower from before the cataclysm that has been a ritual sanctum, an astronomer's laboratory, and a Bluecoat watch post--before its current role as the headquarters of the district's chief gang, the Crows.

landmarks.name: Tangletown
landmarks.description: Hundreds of years ago, one of the massive leviathan hunter ships was partially sunk in the river. Since then, it's collected an attendant flotilla of tiny watercraft, all lashed together into a floating neighborhood. Tangletown is considered neutral ground among the street gangs of Crow's Foot, and no violence is allowed there.

landmarks.name: Strathmill House
landmarks.description: The lost children and unwanted orphans of Crow's Foot inevitably pass through the halls of Strathmill House. Some are cared for and trained for jobs at the docks or the workhouses of Coalridge. Others are quietly instructed in the arts of the lookouts and runners used by the gangs of the district--all for a small fee to Strathmill House, of course.

landmarks.name: Red Sash Sword Academy
landmarks.description: This large mansion has been converted into a training school for the Falling Star style of Iruvian sword play. The Red Sashes, an Iruvian gang who run several luxury drug dens in the district, claim it as their HQ and cover operation for their illicit operations.

districts.scene: Dockers filing to and from work. Minks plying their trade on the corners. A squad of Bluecoats shaking down a shopkeep for a bribe. Rival gangs calling challenges to each other across the rooftops. A fine coach carrying a noble seeking illicit wares.
districts.streets: Multi-level, cramped, dark, foggy. Ash Way, Cinder Street, Rye Street, Candle Street, Hulliver Lane.
districts.buildings: Flophouses, inns, old manors chopped into apartments, traditional stone houses. Smiths, taverns, brothels, and butchers.

npcs.name: Sergeant Lochlan
npcs.role: The senior Bluecoat squad leader in the district, reporting to Captain Dunvil. Lochlan is flexible and reasonable, taking bribes and payoffs when she can; enforcing the law and making examples when necessary.

npcs.name: Lewit, Jol, Myra, Reyf
npcs.role: Bluecoat constables; run an extortion racket.

npcs.name: Mardin Gull
npcs.role: Owner and operator of the Leaky Bucket public house. Mardin was the leader of the Crows many years ago (before Roric and Lyssa) and now enjoys a comfortable retirement out of the scoundrel life.

districts.special_rule: Years of murder have made this the most haunted district. Angry ghosts crave bloodshed here. You may take a Devil's Bargain for +1d for violent action, but the ghost will lash out, too.

districts.name: The Docks
districts.summary: Rough taverns, tattoo parlors, fighting pits, and warehouses.
districts.description: The docks of Doskvol are ancient, going back to the days before the cataclysm, when the area was a colony town of the old Skov kingdom. Today, some commerce has shifted to the new electro-rail lines of the Imperium, but the docks are still bustling with cargo haulers, fishing boats, and the prestigious leviathan hunter ships that provide the raw material that keeps the city running.

landmarks.name: The North Hook Company
landmarks.description: This grand, old-fashioned estate house is headquarters for the oldest surviving shipping and naval exploration enterprise in the Imperium. The North Hook Company has a massive fleet of trade ships and is considered by many to be merely a private front for the Ministry of Preservation. No one knows for sure, since enemies and rivals of the company (not to mention overly curious journalists) tend to disappear.

landmarks.name: Ink Lane
landmarks.description: This twisting back-street is home to many of the city's tattooists as well as several newspapers--who all share the cost of their inks in bulk. A fine place for gossip and rumors of all kinds.

landmarks.name: Saltford's
landmarks.description: A squat stone building that houses one of the more notorious private banks in Duskwall. Being so close to the docks, Saltford's has faced many gangs of whiskey'd sailors that decided to turn to robbery as a new line of work, and defeated them all--sometimes even hanging the corpses from their lamp-posts as discouragement to the next pack of drunken fools.

landmarks.name: The Menagerie
landmarks.description: A fenced-off muddy field, dotted with rusting animal pens, water tanks, and gaudy signage. Sailors traditionally drop off any curious creatures they pick up in their travels, which Captain Rye, the strange proprietor, incorporates into his makeshift zoological displays.

districts.scene: Small and medium steamships docked close, dwarfed by the titanic leviathan hunter ships further out. Throngs of sailors and dockers, doing their work, singing work-songs. Heavy cargo rumbling away on wagons. Shouts and breaking glass from a brawl spilling out of a tavern.
districts.streets: Raised streets perched over the docks themselves, rigged with cranes and winches. North Hook Way, Carter Street, Plume Street, Saltford Street, Ink Lane.
districts.buildings: Massive cargo warehouses. Squat taverns, brothels, and tattoo parlors. Crowded overnight bunkhouses for sailors.

npcs.name: Chief Helker
npcs.role: One of the most influential senior Dockers. Helker has a lot of sway at the docks, and if you cross him, you might find your cargo tossed into the drink--and possibly you along with it.

npcs.name: Tris
npcs.role: A legendary tattooist who only inks those that have looked upon a leviathan and lived to tell the tale. Getting a tattoo from Tris is a rite of passage for everyone who hunts the demons of the Void Sea.

districts.special_rule: Operations against ships at port are considered on "hostile turf " for the purpose of generating heat.

districts.name: Dunslough
districts.summary: A labor camp served by convicts and a ghetto for the destitute poor.
districts.description: Dunslough is a ghetto for the destitute poor of the city, as well as the site of Ironhook Prison and its labor camp. Originally, the ghetto was a neighborhood for families of prisoners, but over the years, extreme poverty and neglect have worn it down into a sodden ruin. A vicious cycle plays out here: crime driven by desperation, then arrest, incarceration, and release back to Dunslough--giving Ironhook an endless supply of laborers to exploit.

landmarks.name: Dunvil Labor Camp
landmarks.description: Poor prisoners who can't afford to bribe the staff at Ironhook spend most of their days toiling at Dunvil Labor Camp, loading precious ores onto barges for the rail station and breaking the larger rocks hauled from the Mire.

landmarks.name: Dunslough Ghetto
landmarks.description: The most destitute of the city end up in Dunslough, working the Mire for a pittance just to buy their daily bread. The city counts the space as "runoff " for the prison grounds, and does nothing to maintain it.

landmarks.name: The Mire
landmarks.description: A massive mud-quarry pit, the Mire is the site of the impact of an ancient celestial body, which left behind a variety of precious ores and jewels embedded in the earth.

districts.scene: Mud-covered laborers returning from the Mire. Destitute families scrounging for scraps along the roadway to the Barrowcleft farms. Bored Ironhook guards, rifles slung on their backs, watching a taskmaster lash a labor camp prisoner.
districts.streets: Cramped, multi-level--some of stone but many of dirt, sodden into thick black mud. No street names to be found.
districts.buildings: Decrepit wooden row houses, many abandoned from fire damage or fallen-in from age. Stone silos, clanking steam machinery, and metal sheds for dredging equipment.

npcs.name: Master Krocket
npcs.role: An unsavory, greasy-haired, scarecrow of a man who runs the snarling pack of vicious dogs used by Ironhook to track down escapees and sniff out contraband and tunnels. His dog-handlers can be found around the labor camp and all about Dunslough, using their status with the prison for favors and bribes.

npcs.name: Vandra
npcs.role: A deathlands scavenger that survived six runs and was pardoned. She knows the landscape beyond the barrier very well--but few can make sense of her haunted mumblings.

districts.name: Nightmarket
districts.summary: The trade center for exotic goods imported by rail. Many vendors trade in illicit goods.
districts.description: Nightmarket is a district dominated by commerce. Situated near Gaddoc Rail Station, Nightmarket receives the bulk of salable goods from the cargo trains that travel across the Imperium, bringing the exotic and rare to Duskwall. The citizens that call Nightmarket home constitute a new class of "elites"--wealthy people who are not of noble descent but nevertheless claim land, status, and power without titles. The district has been taken over by new construction, introducing lavish private townhouses with all of the modern advances for the elites that can afford them.

landmarks.name: The Veil
landmarks.description: A luxurious social club known for its confidentiality and permissive policies regarding guests of arcane or unusual origins. Rolan Volaris, the proprietor and host, is a Tycherosi with an extremely unusual manifestation of his demonic blood: rather than legs, he has the body of a serpent from the waist down... or so people say. Volaris is rarely seen in person.

landmarks.name: Dundridge & Sons
landmarks.description: Considered by many to be the foremost tailor in Duskwall. The Dundridge family has provided the finest clothes and sartorial accoutrements to discerning citizens for over 300 years. Despite their legendary reputation, Dundridge's prices are very reasonable.

landmarks.name: Vreen's Hound Races
landmarks.description: The racing of specially bred hounds is currently in vogue among Doskvol's upper crust. A con man from the Dagger Isles calling himself "Master Vreen" swindled a small fortune from gullible investors to create "the premier hound racing track in the Imperium." The investors have yet to see any returns, but Vreen assures them that a great windfall is due any day now.

landmarks.name: The Devil's Tooth
landmarks.description: A tavern known for its "secret" menu of alchemical concoctions. Adventurous psychonauts may experiment with all manner of mind-altering (or spirit-altering) substances in the relative safety of Mistress Kember's comfortable establishment.

districts.scene: Electric lights in a riot of colors advertise the market stalls of the vendors. Several devout acolytes bow in silent prayer at the statues of the Night Queen, the district's adopted forgotten god. The city's elite, hidden behind masks, slip into the underground to partake of strange pleasures in the private clubs.
districts.streets: Multi-level wooden platforms and boardwalks. Landscaped parks of petrified trees from the deathlands. High-class subterranean avenues. Song Street, String Street, Paper Street, Bell Street.
districts.buildings: Wooden market stalls. Underground stone shops and clubs. Newly constructed private townhouses for the Nightmarket elites.

npcs.name: Jira
npcs.role: A dealer of fine weapons from the Dagger Isles. Greatly respected by many street toughs in the Dusk--a "jira blade" is a status symbol that many aspire to.

npcs.name: Leclure
npcs.role: A purveyor of personal luxuries (soaps, hair oils, perfume, fine silks) who dabbles in fortune telling. Some say that her drowned lover is a ghost that whispers secrets in her ear.

npcs.name: Mordis
npcs.role: A strange merchant that hides its true appearance beneath many layers of robes and hoods. Also fences occult and arcane stolen goods, no questions asked.

districts.special_rule: Nightmarket is the best place to trade illicit and arcane goods in the city, but the darker corners are full of strange horrors. You can take +1d to acquire an asset here, at the cost of 2 stress.

districts.name: Silkshore
districts.summary: The 'red lamp district' and artist community, best navigated by gondola.
districts.description: Criss-crossed by dozens of narrow canals, Silkshore is a district best navigated by gondola, as most visitors to this "red lamp district" do. The brothels, vice dens, food stalls, and exotic shops all perch at the waterside, ready to satisfy the appetites of their clientele, no questions asked. Silkshore is a place of public indulgence and private indelicacy, catering to every pleasure imaginable--as well as some that strain the bounds of fantasy.

landmarks.name: The Spark Grounds
landmarks.description: Beneath the crackling flares of a lightning tower, this open field draws crowds to watch the death-defying stunts of acrobats and circus performers, including the notorious "spark flyers" who soar in manned kites adorned with bits of metal to attract arcs of electricity from the barrier in a pyrotechnic display.

landmarks.name: The Ease
landmarks.description: The northern part of the district is more easily navigated by gondola than by coach, and the dozens of entryways into the canals are flanked by shops and brothels advertising their wares with colored electric lights and flying banners.

landmarks.name: Fogcrest
landmarks.description: A high, peaked hilltop crowded with rowhouses jammed within the maze of narrow stairways that constitute its "streets." A bohemian community of artists, free-thinkers, psychedelic explorers, and philosophers.

landmarks.name: Ankhayat Park
landmarks.description: This extravagantly landscaped space is the largest open area in the city, hosting many public festivals and events throughout the year. The Iruvian noble for whom the park is named keeps a falcon aviary and stable of fine horses at the park and sometimes organizes gamehawking for the nobility.

districts.scene: Hundreds of gondolas gliding to and fro in the spiderweb of canals, carrying eager patrons to the bounty of pleasure-houses and vice dens. Artists and philosophers arguing epistemology over Iruvian tea at the cafes on Fogcrest hill.
districts.streets: The few remaining surface streets are interrupted at every turn by a bridge over a tiny canal. Colored lights and patterned silks hang roof to roof, signaling various indugences in a complex code for the initiated. Greenwater, Highwater, Chimewater, Ringwater, Sweetwater.
districts.buildings: Tall, narrow rowhouses of wood and stone. Low canal-side shacks and stalls. Cramped old stone houses and converted offices on Fogcrest hill.

npcs.name: Levyra
npcs.role: A medium who invites clients to bring ghosts in bottles to possess her so they can share a few final words before the ghost is "freed" (Levyra hands it off to the waiting Spirit Wardens nearby).

npcs.name: Helene
npcs.role: The elegant and mysterious proprietor of the Silver Stag Casino. People say she would have been a queen of Severos had she lived in the old days before the Empire.

npcs.name: Madame Tesslyn
npcs.role: Operates the Red Lamp brothel, the oldest and most respected institution of its sort in the city.

districts.special_rule: Should you overindulge your vice while in Silkshore, you'll get a taste for it. Take +1d to your roll the next time you indulge your vice here.

districts.name: Six Towers
districts.summary: A formerly rich district, now worn down and dilapidated.
districts.description: This formerly prestigious district has faded over the centuries into a pale shadow of what it once was. The eponymous six towers were originally the grand residences of Doskvol's first noble families. All but two (Bowmore House and Rowan House) have been sold off and converted into cheap apartments or fallen into ruin and abandoned. The district has an empty, haunted feel, with many sprawling old buildings dark without power, broad stone streets cracked and buckled, and the fires of squatters crackling from overgrown lots.

landmarks.name: Rowan House
landmarks.description: One of the last of the original six towers, this antique building resembles an ancient castle from history books, complete with moat, draw-bridge, and arrow-slit windows. The powerful Rowan family rules their holdings from within the fortress, rarely venturing beyond the security of its thick stone walls.

landmarks.name: Mistshore Park
landmarks.description: This dark and overgrown space overlooks the eastern branch of the river Dosk and the deathlands beyond. In old folk ballads, young lovers who could not be together would commit suicide in this park. Whatever the truth of it, the park is certainly haunted now.

landmarks.name: Scurlock Manor
landmarks.description: The Scurlock family came to Duskwall centuries ago and was once a great force in the city, before some curse or calamity befell their line. This tumble-down manor house and tangle of vines is all that remains of their original fortune. It's said that a young nephew or cousin still resides there, but Lord Scurlock himself has moved on to finer abodes.

landmarks.name: Arms of the Weeping Lady
landmarks.description: This grand building, formerly an opera house, is now a soup-kitchen and bunkhouse for the destitute, run by the charity of the Weeping Lady. Locals use this landmark as the demarcation between the districts of Charterhall and Six Towers.

districts.scene: Bits of trash, blown by a cold wind, skitter across empty streets, illuminated only by a few still-working street lamps and the campfires of squatters. The shutters and doors of abandoned buildings moan, creak, and bang in a haunted chorus. Residents hustle by, heads down, clutching spiritbane charms close to their breasts.
districts.streets: Broad stone avenues, cracked and broken, dark without power; overgrown and neglected. Comber Way, Bowmore Way, Rowan Way, Coleburn Avenue.
districts.buildings: Palatial estates, tumbled into disrepair. Grand manors, remodeled into cramped and cheap apartments.

npcs.name: Mother Narya
npcs.role: Runs the Arms of the Weeping Lady charity house.

npcs.name: Chef Roselle
npcs.role: One of the best cooks in the city, still operating the legendary Golden Plum restaurant--worth the trip into the haunted streets of Six Towers.

npcs.name: Flint
npcs.role: A spirit trafficker who trades out of a condemned manor house.

districts.special_rule: The many empty buildings and abandoned properties make this district a perfect location for a hidden scoundrel's lair.

districts.name: Whitecrown
districts.summary: The sprawling estates of the Lord Governor, Hunter Commander, Master Warden, and Doskvol Academy.
districts.description: Whitecrown sits atop a grand peak on the island across North Hook channel from the city proper. From this lofty height, the Lord Governor's stronghold oversees all, flanked by the grand estates of the most powerful nobility and the extravagantly appointed campus of Doskvol Academy. Whitecrown is a rich and rarefied world unto itself--most citizens live out their entire lives in the city without ever once crossing the bridge to the glittering spires of wealth and power there.

landmarks.name: Lord Governor's Stronghold
landmarks.description: The Emperor originally commissioned this stronghold as a garrison for the Imperial Military stationed at North Hook prior to the invasion of Skovlan. It now houses the Lord Governor, their family, and governmental aides as well.

landmarks.name: Doskvol Academy
landmarks.description: Hailed as one of the finer institutions of learning in the Empire, the school is most wellknown as the instructional facility for the leviathan hunter captains and their senior officers. Training cruises for new recruits are conducted year-round to replace the poor souls lost in the hunts.

landmarks.name: Master Warden's Estate
landmarks.description: This gigantic, fortified manor is home to the Commander of the Spirit Wardens and is their primary training facility. It's said that some spirits are not destroyed at Bellweather--but are brought here instead for some unknown purpose.

landmarks.name: North Hook Lighthouse
landmarks.description: This ancient structure has been converted into an electro-plasmic apparatus capable of providing a navigation beacon for hundreds of miles into the darkness of the Void Sea around Duskwall.

districts.scene: Imperial soldiers parade outside the stronghold, astride their armored steeds, gleaming lances held high. Trainee crews run drills on a leviathan hunter ship docked for refitting. The lavish carriages and electroplasmic coaches of the fabulously wealthy glide by, carrying their privileged passengers to luxurious destinations.
districts.streets: Broad, polished stone, brightly lit to near daylight by a riot of warm electric lights. Whitecrown Boulevard, Swancrest Avenue.
districts.buildings: Grand, elegant facades; landscaped terraces, balconies, and elevated walkways connecting bright marble buildings with inlaid platinum and gold details.

npcs.name: Maestro Helleren
npcs.role: Senior composer and conductor of the Spiregarden Theater, premiere performance venue for the elite of the city.

npcs.name: Lady Freyla
npcs.role: Regarded by some as the finest sommelier in the Empire. She serves only the most deserving at the Emperor's Cask.

districts.special_rule: Most engagement rolls suffer -2d due to heavy Bluecoat patrols. Operations against the nobility in Whitecrown are considered on "hostile turf " for the purpose of generating heat.

districts.name: The Deathlands
districts.summary: The wasteland beyond the lightning barriers.
districts.description: Beyond the lightning barriers, the world is a wasteland of petrified trees, ash, and choking clouds of miasma. Restless ghosts ceaselessly search for the faintest spark of life essence upon which to prey.

districts.name: Eeleries & Farms
districts.summary: Eel farms, mushroom caverns, and radiant-energy crops outside the city.
districts.description: About half of Doskvol's food needs are met by imports; the other half is gathered locally from eel farms, mushroom caverns, and crops nurtured by wondrous radiant energy lamps.

districts.name: Gaddoc Rail Station
districts.description: Electro-rail trains from across the Imperium arrive here daily with goods and passengers.

districts.name: Ironhook
districts.description: A towering metal fortress, where the worst (or most unlucky) criminals are incarcerated. Many are forced into labor in the Southern fields and pit-mines of Dunslough. The condemned are sent to scavenge in the deathlands.

districts.name: The Lost District
districts.summary: A once-wealthy area abandoned to the deathlands.
districts.description: A once wealthy area, ravaged by plague then abandoned to the deathlands when the second lightning barrier was built. Contains many lost treasures for the foolhardy to seek out.

districts.name: Old North Port
districts.summary: An obsolete port outside the lightning barrier.
districts.description: Before the deluge of the second century, the Dosk River delta was only a shallow bog surrounding a coal mining settlement. Ships used the Old North Port to dock and re-supply before crossing the open sea to Skovlan. The obsolete port was abandoned to the deathlands when the second lightning barrier was built.

districts.name: The Void Sea
districts.description: During the cataclysm, the oceans turned to black ink. Tiny points of light, arrayed like constellations of stars, can be seen far below the surface. Only the bravest or most desperate of captains dare to sail beyond sight of land, into a sea as black and dark as the sky above. The vengeful ghosts that plague the land tend to shun the open ocean, but there are more terrible things prowling the deeps.

factions.name: The Billhooks

npcs.name: Tarvul
npcs.role: Leader (serving life in prison; savage, arrogant, family man)

factions.summary: A tough gang of thugs who prefer hatchets, meat hooks, and pole arms.
factions.turf: A butcher shop (HQ), stockyard, and slaughterhouse. Animal fighting pits and gambling dens. Several terrified merchants and businesses, which they extort.
factions.assets: A large gang of bloodthirsty butchers. A pack of death-dogs.
factions.quirks: The Billhooks have a bloody reputation, often leaving the butchered corpses of their victims strewn about in a grisly display. Many wonder why the Bluecoats turn a blind eye to their savagery.
factions.situation: Erin and Coran both want to take control of the Billhooks gang, either when Tarvul gets too old or by taking the position by force. Meanwhile, the rest of the gang wants to continue their reign of terror to pressure a magistrate to pardon Tarvul and other gang members from Ironhook.

clocks.name: Terrorize magistrates to pardon members in prison

factions.name: Bluecoats
factions.summary: The City Watch of Duskwall. Known as the meanest gang in the city. Corrupt, violent, and cruel.
factions.turf: The Bluecoats claim the whole city as their turf, but find their influence severely limited in Whitecrown, where the Imperial Military garrison holds sway under command of the Lord Governor.
factions.assets: Many large gangs of vicious thugs in uniform. Armored coaches and canal patrol boats. Public punishment sites (pillories, stocks, hanging cages).
factions.quirks: The Bluecoats are divided into companies by district, and they have fierce rivalries, encouraged by their superiors — often good-natured, but sometimes violent.
factions.situation: The Bluecoats have become jealous of the elite hardware and vehicles used by the Imperial Military. They want to refit their watch-guards in heavy armor and weapons, to better strike fear into those they prey upon.

clocks.name: Procure bigger budget, military arms & equipment

factions.name: The Brigade
factions.summary: The fire-fighters of the city. Beloved for their life-saving heroism, or reviled for their looting and extortion rackets. Also known as 'Sallies' (from 'salamanders,' their ancient name).
factions.notes: Index-only entry — no detailed write-up in the rulebook.

factions.name: Cabbies
factions.summary: The public coach operators. They also breed the large Akorosian goats used to pull the carriages. An impressive gossip network.

factions.name: The Church of the Ecstasy of the Flesh
factions.summary: The 'state religion' honors the life of the body and abhors the corrupted spirit world. Essentially a secret society.
factions.turf: The Sanctorium grand cathedral in Brightstone. Many other smaller temples across the city.
factions.assets: A large treasury of tithes from citizens. Extensive arcane and occult libraries, workspaces, and artifacts. Many cohorts of acolytes and hollows who enforce the will of the Church's leadership.
factions.quirks: Zealous believers volunteer to be hollowed to 'become purified.' This was once common among the ancient cult of the Empty Vessel, which preceded the Church.
factions.situation: The purest beings (according to secret teachings of the Church) are those entirely without spirits: the demons. The most devout of the Church seek to become as they are, to unlock the secret of ascension. Many dark experiments and rituals with hulls, hollows, vampires — and the rare demon — are conducted in the labyrinthine dungeons below the Sanctorium.

factions.name: The Circle of Flame
factions.summary: A refined secret society of antiquarians and scholars; cover for extortion, graft, vice, and murder.
factions.turf: The Centuralia club, Six Towers (HQ).
factions.assets: Vast treasury provided by wealthy membership. Impressive collection of ancient artifacts, maps, and ephemera. Highly trained and discreet private security force.
factions.quirks: One of The Seven is actually a demon in disguise.
factions.situation: The Circle has an extensive library of scholarly works that catalog many of the arcane artifacts and valuable treasures that disappeared when the Lost District was abandoned. Of special interest are the remains of Kotar — a legendary sorcerer, demon, or hero. The Eye, Hand, and Heart of Kotar are said to possess great power.

clocks.name: Acquire all the ancient artifacts of Kotar

factions.name: Citizens of Barrowcleft
factions.summary: The clannish farm families who work the radiant-energy fields across the river.

factions.name: Citizens of Brightstone
factions.summary: The wealthiest and most influential citizens of Doskvol.

factions.name: Citizens of Charhollow
factions.summary: The bulk of Doskvol's working-class workforce — servants, dockers, sailors, and stockyard hands.

factions.name: Citizens of Charterhall
factions.summary: City officials, university scholars, and captains of Imperial industry.

factions.name: Citizens of Coalridge
factions.summary: The machinists and industrial laborers of the city's factories.

factions.name: Citizens of Crow's Foot
factions.summary: A patchwork populace caught in the crossfire of competing gangs.

factions.name: Citizens of Dunslough
factions.summary: The destitute poor of the city, ground down by the prison camp's cycle.

factions.name: Citizens of Nightmarket
factions.summary: A new class of non-noble elites who built fortunes on rail-borne trade.

factions.name: Citizens of Silkshore
factions.summary: The proprietors, performers, and patrons of the red-lamp district.

factions.name: Citizens of Six Towers
factions.summary: Squatters and faded gentry holding out in a once-grand district.

factions.name: Citizens of the Docks
factions.summary: Dockers, sailors, fishers, and leviathan-hunter crews of the port.

factions.name: Citizens of Whitecrown
factions.summary: The Lord Governor's court and the highest-ranking noble families of the city.

factions.name: City Council
factions.summary: The elite nobility who run the city government, its treasury, magistrates, and public works.
factions.turf: City council chambers in Charterhall, attendant government offices, and impregnable city treasury vaults. Owns all public spaces — streets, docks, waterways.
factions.assets: A massive treasury of coin and valuable goods. Many officials, barristers, clerks. The public coaches operated by the Cabbies.
factions.quirks: All councilors are high-ranking adepts in the Church of the Ecstasy of the Flesh. Some are also secretly initiates in the Path of Echoes.
factions.situation: Three councilors (Bowmore, Clelland, Rowan) have aligned against Strangford and are maneuvering to remove the house from the council. Dunvil and Penderyn have not taken sides. Will the conspirators arrange for the necessary scandal, framed crime, or assassinations? Or can Strangford House stand against them and eliminate the threats?

factions.name: The Crows
factions.summary: An old gang with new leadership. Known for running illegal games of chance and extortion rackets.
factions.turf: Claims all of Crow's Foot as their turf. HQ in an abandoned City Watch tower. Operates gambling dens in Crow's Foot and extortion rackets at the Docks.
factions.assets: A veteran gang of thugs and killers. A number of small boats. A fortified HQ.
factions.quirks: Roric's body was lost during his murder (it fell into a canal). His vengeful ghost is now at large in the city.
factions.situation: Lyssa murdered the former boss of the Crows, Roric. She is a fearsome killer, but her position as leader is uncertain. Some were very loyal to Roric. As the power-play continues, the Crows' hold on the district just might slip away.

factions.name: Cyphers
factions.summary: The messenger guild of the city. Cyphers swear sacred oaths of secrecy — never revealing the contents of their messages or the identities of their clients — or so they claim.

factions.name: Dagger Isles Consulate
factions.summary: Diplomatic mission of the Dagger Isles in Doskvol.

factions.name: Deathlands Scavengers
factions.summary: Convicts from Ironhook and desperate freelancers who roam the wasteland beyond the lightning barriers.
factions.turf: A few precious hold-fasts in the deathlands, secured by ancient rites against spirits. Hunting grounds to feed on the few strange animals that survived the cataclysm.
factions.assets: Generators, lightning hooks, gas-masks, air tanks, and other essentials of deathlands survival. A secret ancient book of ritual sorcery.
factions.quirks: Possession is a common hazard, and scavengers either learn to deal with it, or go mad and vanish into the darkness of the wastes. Lady Thorn's company has adapted well.
factions.situation: Condemned prisoners are sometimes given 'mercy' and sent into the deathlands rather than executed at Ironhook. A few survive thanks to Lady Thorn, who trains them in the ways of deathlands hunting and survival. The scavengers hunt for lost artifacts and treasures, sometimes for enough to buy a pardon.
factions.notes: Operates outside the lightning barriers — no in-city district turf.

factions.name: The Dimmer Sisters

npcs.name: Roslyn
npcs.role: Servant (patient, loyal, arcane); deals with outside contacts

factions.summary: House-bound recluses with an occult reputation.
factions.turf: Fine old manor house and grounds (HQ), as well as the ancient temple ruin and subterranean canal beneath. Apothecaries and witches in their service.
factions.assets: A private electroplasmic generator, lightning barriers, and spirit containment vessels. Many spirits bound to service.
factions.quirks: The precise number of sisters is unknown. Some say they are an ancient family of possessing spirits. Others say they are vampires. Everyone knows that if you go into their house, you never come out again.
factions.situation: The Sisters have been slowly and secretly consolidating the trade of captured spirits and spirit essences in Doskvol for several decades. Only a few rivals stand between them and domination of the market.

factions.name: Dockers
factions.summary: The hard-bitten laborers who work the docks.

factions.name: The Fog Hounds
factions.summary: A crew of rough smugglers looking for a patron.
factions.turf: Underground canal dock (HQ). North and East city canal routes. Northern Void Sea routes. Old North Port supply caches.
factions.assets: Medium steamship, Fog Hound. A crew of hard-bitten, tough, expert sailors — all former Void Sea transport haulers. A wide array of Imperial transport and cargo documents, some forged, some legit.
factions.quirks: As veterans of many cruises on the Void Sea, Vale and her crew can be insular and clannish, with low initial opinions of outsiders. Once won, however, their loyalty is rock solid and fierce.
factions.situation: Vale and her crew have mastered the Northern smuggling routes in and out of Duskwall. They're absorbing or eliminating their few remaining rivals and seeking a steady patron who needs reliable contraband.

factions.name: The Forgotten Gods
factions.summary: Cults that attempt to follow the old ways from before the cataclysm, doing the bidding of demons and darker things. There are many cults, who rarely organize together. An individual cult is usually Tier I or Tier II.
factions.notes: Scattered across the city; no single turf.

factions.name: The Foundation
factions.summary: The powerful ancient order of architects and builders. Many of their enemies have disappeared behind the brick and mortar of Doskvol.

factions.name: Gondoliers
factions.summary: The canal boat operators. Venerated by ancient tradition. Said to know occult secrets (many things are submerged in the Dusk).
factions.turf: The canals of Doskvol. Even the Bluecoats' canal patrol pays respect to them.
factions.assets: Fleet of gondolas and other water-craft. Map of known spirit wells and arcane sites across the city. A dedicated cohort of Adepts.
factions.quirks: Initiation into the Gondoliers grants the Whisper's Compel special ability.
factions.situation: Killers have disposed of bodies in the canals for centuries; the vengeful ghosts that rise are a serious threat dealt with by the Gondoliers since ancient times. Beloved by most citizens, who prefer them to the ruthless Spirit Wardens. A sudden influx of ritually disfigured hollows dumped in the canals has sparked Gondolier investigation (the Spirit Wardens are pointedly ignoring the situation).

factions.name: The Gray Cloaks
factions.summary: A crew of former Bluecoats turned to crime after being framed and expelled from the City Watch.
factions.turf: The basement of a burned-down City Watch station (HQ). Several apartments above a tobacconist in Six Towers. A pit-fighting arena and gambling den.
factions.assets: A sizeable gang of former Bluecoats turned enforcers. Old uniforms and badges, often used to pass as the City Watch.
factions.situation: All former Bluecoats framed for a crime committed by their station commander. They were skimming and taking bribes like everyone else, but they didn't burn down the station to destroy evidence in the case against Lord Strangford (Leviathan Hunters). Several inspectors know the truth but can't prove it — yet. Lord Strangford would pay well to have these loose ends removed permanently.

clocks.name: Secure Six Towers as their turf

factions.name: The Grinders
factions.summary: A vicious gang of former dockers and leviathan blood refinery workers from Skovlan.
factions.turf: Abandoned dock warehouse (HQ) and underground canal dock.
factions.assets: A few small canal boats. Wrecking tools and explosives.
factions.quirks: Many Grinders have been mutated by the toxic rain that plagues Lockport.
factions.situation: Lockport processes 90% of the demon blood siphoned by the leviathan hunter ships of Doskvol. The huge refineries have poisoned the city under toxic fumes and acid rain. A group of dockers and refinery workers have come to Doskvol to raise an army and seize a warship to destroy the Empire's refineries. To raise funds, they've turned to smash & grab looting and hijacking cargo barges.

clocks.name: Raise a crew, steal a war ship

factions.name: The Hive
factions.summary: A guild of legitimate merchants who secretly trade in contraband. Named for their symbol, a golden bee.
factions.turf: Many shops, taverns, cafes, warehouses, and other mercantile establishments all across the city. No centralized HQ.
factions.assets: A massive treasury. Elite mercenaries on retainer. A fleet of transport ships, carriages, wagons, and private trains.
factions.quirks: The Hive is known to avoid doing business with any occult or arcane groups. The Church of Ecstasy is popular among Hive members, who reject the superstitions of the past.
factions.situation: Djera Maha grew up an urchin in the Dagger Isles and learned the secrets of vice and smuggling working her way up the trade routes. With her acquisition and distribution network in the city (and within the Ministry of Preservation), she is poised to take over the contraband markets. Maha had a close relationship — some say romantic — with Roric, leader of the Crows, who was recently murdered by his second-in-command.

factions.name: The Horde
factions.summary: A mass of hollows, all united in some fell purpose, controlled by an unknown power.
factions.notes: No fixed turf — moves through the city as a roving threat.

factions.name: Imperial Military
factions.summary: The armed forces of the Imperium stationed in Doskvol. Garrisons are posted at Gaddoc Rail Station, aboard the naval destroyer Paragon, and at the Lord Governor's stronghold (about 250 troops in total).

factions.name: Ink Rakes
factions.summary: The journalists, muckrakers, and newspaper publishers of Doskvol.

factions.name: Inspectors
factions.summary: The criminal investigators of the City Watch. They have a reputation for ethics and integrity (no one likes them). They present evidence for prosecutions to the city magistrates.

factions.name: Ironhook Prison
factions.summary: Where many scoundrels spend the bulk of their lives. Several criminal organizations are run by convicts inside its walls.

factions.name: Iruvian Consulate
factions.summary: Diplomatic mission of the Iruvian Empire in Doskvol.

factions.name: Laborers
factions.summary: The general workforce of the city — factory hands, eelery workers, mill operators, and field hands.

factions.name: The Lampblacks
factions.summary: The former lamp-lighter guild, turned to crime when their services were replaced by electric lights.
factions.turf: HQ in the office of a coal warehouse. Operates a handful of brothels and cheap drug dens across Crow's Foot.
factions.assets: A fearsome gang of leg-breakers and mayhem-makers. A number of smugglers on the payroll who run their drugs.
factions.quirks: Bazso Baz is a member of a secret society (forgotten gods cult, 'The Empty Vessel') and sometimes puts the needs of that group ahead of the well-being of his gang.
factions.situation: The Lampblacks and the Red Sashes are at war over turf and vengeance for deaths on both sides. Bazso Baz is recruiting every free blade in the district. The Lampblacks are not particularly well-connected politically, but are akin to folk-heroes among the working class.

clocks.name: Become ward boss of Crow's Foot

factions.name: Leviathan Hunters
factions.summary: The captains and crews that grapple with titanic demons of the Void Sea to drain their blood for processing into electroplasm.
factions.turf: Massive metal docks for the huge hunter ships and associated construction and repair facilities. Several small private leviathan blood processing facilities for the captains' personal shares.
factions.assets: The leviathan hunter fleet (each vessel owned by the noble house that built and commands it). Many cohorts of expert sailors, spark-craft technicians, demonologist Whispers, void-touched navigators. Companies of marines.
factions.situation: The captains have a horrible secret: the known hunting grounds for leviathans are coming up barren. The immortal creatures, once so reliable in their movements, have begun to migrate elsewhere. New hunting grounds must be found before the surplus disappears, and with it, the lightning barriers and the survival of the human race.

factions.name: The Lost
factions.summary: A group of street-toughs and ex-soldiers dedicated to protecting the downtrodden and the hopeless.
factions.turf: Converted rail car (HQ). The poverty-stricken streets of Coalridge and Dunslough.
factions.assets: A very experienced gang of formerly vicious thugs, killers, and Imperial soldiers.
factions.quirks: All members have done horrible things in their former lives and believe they must atone for these 'sins.' Each keeps a pile of stones under their bed — one for each sin balanced with a just deed.
factions.situation: Currently focusing efforts in Coalridge, running a campaign of sabotage, terror, and savage beatings against the most cruel workhouse foremen. The local Bluecoat patrols are starting to complain to their commanders. Meanwhile, foremen are paying top dollar for anyone who'll take the Lost out of the picture.

factions.name: Ministry of Preservation
factions.summary: The governmental body of the Imperium that oversees all transportation between cities and the disbursement of food and other vital resources.
factions.turf: The electro-rail train lines of the Imperium. Radiant energy farms, eeleries, and other food-growing enterprises throughout the city.
factions.assets: A fleet of cargo ships and their armed escorts. A significant treasury from taxation and transportation licensing. The Rail Jacks who work the train lines. A private mercenary company.
factions.situation: The Ministry leadership believes that the leviathan hunters are too vital to be controlled by bickering noble houses. Agents within the ministry have been tasked with espionage, sabotage, and political actions to ultimately seize control of the hunters and bring them into Ministry control.

factions.name: The Path of Echoes
factions.summary: A mystery cult that borders on open rebellion against spirit laws. They revere the ancients, and seek to gain knowledge from the past — including consorting with ghosts.
factions.notes: Operates secretly across the city; no public turf.

factions.name: Rail Jacks
factions.summary: The men and women who protect the electro-trains of the Imperium from the savage spirits of the deathlands. Have recently been making noises about forming a union.

factions.name: The Reconciled
factions.summary: An association of ancient spirits who have not gone feral with the passage of time.
factions.turf: None.
factions.assets: Several secret and hidden spirit wells across the city and in the deathlands, which give the Reconciled the arcane energy they need to survive.
factions.quirks: The spirits of the Reconciled do not lose their minds or become obsessed with vengeance as other spirits do. They can possess a victim indefinitely without any adverse effects.
factions.situation: The Reconciled are very ancient and wise; they see themselves as the rightful and just rulers that Duskwall needs. A few City Council members have become initiates in the Path of Echoes and will soon be vulnerable to possession. These councilors are also high-ranking members of the Church of Ecstasy, opening an infiltration into that organization as well.

factions.name: The Red Sashes
factions.summary: Originally a school of ancient Iruvian sword arts, since expanded into criminal endeavors.
factions.turf: HQ in their sword-fighting school/temple. Operates a handful of high-end drug dens across Crow's Foot and the Docks.
factions.assets: Small contingent of master sword-fighters. Master alchemist; many potent potions and essences.
factions.quirks: Several members are sons and daughters of Iruvian nobility and diplomats in Doskvol. They train in swordplay at the school and sometimes participate in gang activities. Their families will commit significant resources to punishing anyone who harms their children.
factions.situation: The Red Sashes and the Lampblacks are at war over turf and vengeance. Mylera is recruiting every free blade in the district. The Red Sashes are very well-connected — former students placed at the Iruvian Consulate, in the Path of Echoes, and among the Inspectors.

clocks.name: Become ward boss of Crow's Foot

factions.name: Sailors
factions.summary: The captains and crews for merchant and Imperial Navy ships.

factions.name: Lord Scurlock

npcs.name: Lord Scurlock
npcs.role: (enigmatic, cold, arcane, old-fashioned) — counts as a faction unto himself; in conflicts he counts as a large gang (20 people)

factions.summary: An ancient noble, said to be immortal, like the Emperor. Possibly a vampire or sorcerer. Obsessed with the occult.
factions.turf: A secret lair outside the city. A dilapidated manor house in Six Towers and the catacombs beneath. An array of business holdings and cult shrines across the city, collected for some united purpose known only to Scurlock.
factions.assets: An impressive collection of occult and arcane curios, books, and ephemera. An ancient demonic temple.
factions.quirks: Scurlock is immune to spirits. Ghosts can't see, hear, or harm him. He makes no sound when he moves and is sometimes difficult to look at directly.
factions.situation: Lord Scurlock is bound by ancient magic to the demon Setarra. Now Scurlock must fulfill a debt: Setarra has found a nest of sea demons in the harbor, encased in stone, chained by magic from the cataclysm. She seeks to free them. Scurlock will aid her in this or suffer a dark doom.

factions.name: Servants
factions.summary: Domestic servants in the great houses of the city's elite. A quiet but well-placed gossip network.

factions.name: Severosi Consulate
factions.summary: Diplomatic mission of Severos in Doskvol.

factions.name: The Silver Nails
factions.summary: A company of Severosi mercenaries who fought for the Empire in the Unity War. Renowned ghost killers.
factions.turf: A large inn (The Mustang) and its fine stables (HQ).
factions.assets: A contingent of exquisite Severosian cavalry horses — fearless, swift, and trained to hunt and battle spirits. Arcane lances.
factions.quirks: Each member wears a ring fashioned from a silver nail, which protects against possession. They're trained in the Ghost Fighter special ability (Cutter).
factions.situation: Thanks to their expertise riding in the deathlands of Severos, the Silver Nails are perfectly suited to explore the forbidden Lost District outside the lightning barrier. Once the fiercest ghosts are driven out, they can seize control and plunder the forgotten treasures within. (The Spirit Wardens currently control access and keep the Silver Nails — and everyone else — out.)

clocks.name: Destroy spirits in the Lost District

factions.name: Skovlan Consulate
factions.summary: Diplomatic mission of Skovlan in Doskvol — strained after the Unity War.

factions.name: Skovlander Refugees
factions.summary: Desperate survivors of the Unity War, forced to criminal opportunities when denied all others.

factions.name: Sparkwrights

npcs.name: Una Farros
npcs.role: Instructor at Charterhall University (curious, vain, famous)

factions.summary: The engineers who maintain the lightning barriers. Also pioneers of spark-craft technology, indulging in dangerous research.
factions.turf: Massive workshop, factory, and design facility in Coalridge.
factions.assets: The electroplasmic generators, city lights, lightning barriers and associated facilities and systems across the city.
factions.situation: For centuries the Sparkwrights have worked in secret to develop an alternative fuel that could replace leviathan blood. A few researchers have gotten close, but 'accidents' have inevitably killed them and destroyed their work — certainly arranged by the nobility who rule because of their stranglehold on leviathan hunting. There is always a daring visionary willing to try again — at the risk of their life.

factions.name: Spirit Wardens

npcs.name: Bakoros
npcs.role: Code-name for one or more Wardens; sometimes lectures at the College of Immortal Studies

factions.summary: The bronze-masked hunters who destroy rogue spirits. Also run Bellweather Crematorium to properly dispose of corpses.
factions.turf: Bellweather Crematorium. The Master Warden's estate in Whitecrown.
factions.assets: The death bells that ring whenever someone dies in the city, and the deathseeker crows that fly to find the body (ancient, arcane). Many cohorts of expert Whispers. The most advanced spectrological and spark-craft equipment, including several spirit-hunter hulls.
factions.quirks: Membership is secret and utterly anonymous. They cut all ties and have no families or close relationships, save their fellow Wardens.
factions.situation: The Spirit Wardens know an enemy is attempting to infiltrate their ranks (they don't yet know it's the Unseen). They are laying a trap to uncover their identity and eliminate them.

factions.name: Ulf Ironborn
factions.summary: A brutal Skovlander, newly arrived in the Dusk, fighting everyone for turf.
factions.turf: Rooms, workshop, and stable at The Old Forge tavern (HQ). A gambling den.
factions.assets: A small but powerfully savage gang of thugs.
factions.quirks: As a refugee of the Unity War, Ulf does not trust the local Akorosi, or anyone with strong allegiance to the Imperial government. Those of Skovlander blood find it easy to win his trust.
factions.situation: Ulf is newly arrived in Doskvol, seeking his fortune. His gang has had recent success with savage smash & grab operations, leading into a potential 'protection' racket. As more Skovlander refugees swell the population, the bigotry of locals is starting to surface. Ulf's blind rage will be sparked off when he encounters this, leading his gang into war with any 'true Duskers' brave enough to stand up to him.

factions.name: The Unseen

npcs.name: Grull
npcs.role: Mid-level thug with big ambitions; undercover as a coach driver

factions.summary: An insidious criminal enterprise with secret membership. Thought to pull the strings of the entire underworld.
factions.turf: A multitude of vice dens and extortion rackets across the city — virtually none realize that they pay up to the Unseen. Several opulent townhouses used as safe houses.
factions.assets: A legion of thugs, thieves, and killers on-call to their secret masters.
factions.quirks: The perfect secrecy of the Unseen is the result of arcane rituals. Core members can recognize each other with attuned second sight. Any non-member who learns the identity of a member falls victim to a ritual that removes that memory from their mind after a few moments.
factions.situation: The Unseen crave the power and authority of the Spirit Wardens, whose own secret membership has so far resisted infiltration. The Tower and The Star plot to place their own spies and operatives among the Wardens and seize it from within.

factions.name: The Weeping Lady

npcs.name: Mother Narya
npcs.role: Runs the Arms of the Weeping Lady charity house (Kind, Patient)

factions.summary: A charity and pseudo-religion, honoring the first Lord Governor of Doskvol, Lady Devera, said to be a champion of the poor.

factions.name: The Wraiths
factions.summary: A mysterious crew of masked thieves and spies.
factions.turf: Silkshore and Nightmarket are their primary hunting grounds. They specialize in the theft of luxury items and intelligence gathering for clients to use as blackmail.
factions.assets: A scattered collection of secret rooftop shelters. A secret lair in a tower in Silkshore. All manner of thieves' gear for burglary.
factions.quirks: Each member wears a mask and conceals their true identity with an alias. They communicate with a private sign language.
factions.situation: The Wraiths recently completed a heist at a luxury brothel in Nightmarket and happened to grab the private map book of a leviathan hunter — showing the secret hunting grounds of augured leviathan sites that will be used by the ship Storm Palace next season. Useless to the Wraiths, but worth a small fortune to another leviathan hunter. They are quietly arranging a sale.
